I will not seek a trace,
(or, as we rather say in English,
to escape me: (Har pp. 120 and 174: [this latter rendering being agreeable with explanations of

which will be found in a later part of this paragraph:]) i. e. I will not leave the thing when I see it ocularly, and seek the trace or vestige, thereof, after its [the thing's] disappearing from me: and the origin of it was the fact that a man saw the slayer of his brother, and when he desired to slay him, he [the latter] said, “ I will ransom myself with a hundred she-camels; ” whereupon he [the other] said,
; and slew him: (TA:) it is a prov., thus, or, as some relate it,
